round2: Round 2 flew by, making some of your game predictions look like pure fiction. Jaclyn and Mom actually showed up, nailing multiple picks, while M.C.B. and Stephanie's unwavering faith in Edmonton and Anaheim proved they prefer fantasy over reality.
round3: Another round, another chance for most of you to prove how little you know. Everyone picked the Rangers to win, so congrats on that collective faceplant. At least Jaclyn and Dad actually paid attention to the West, nailing Detroit in 6 while the rest of you fell for Colorado.
round4: Round 4 wrapped up with Detroit sweeping Philly in four games. For those of you who picked the Flyers, congratulations on predicting a longer series than reality, and the wrong winner. Jaclyn and Mom correctly picked the champion, though their game counts suggest they were hoping for a bit more hockey before the inevitable.
round1: Alright, Round 1 is over, and it seems some of you are better at picking lint off a sweater than playoff winners. While everyone correctly nailed the Rangers and Avs, Dad managed to pick the right number of games for the wrong team twice, a truly unique talent. Meanwhile, Mom's bracket is so busted, she might as well have picked the Zamboni driver to win the Cup.
overall: Alright, the 1997 playoffs are officially in the books, and the Detroit Red Wings finally hoisted the Cup, which means Jaclyn is officially hoisting the pool trophy after a truly dominant performance. She ran away with it, racking up 61 points with ten correct teams and nailing eight series lengths, making the rest of you look like you were picking names out of a hat. Derrick put up a decent fight for second with 52 points, proving he at least *tried*, but the real standout was Marc, who managed a truly spectacular low of 16 points despite picking nine teams, getting only one series length correct – a performance so baffling it suggests he might have been betting on the wrong sport entirely. Seriously, Marc, did you even watch a single game, or just scribble down names you thought sounded cool? Congratulations, Jaclyn; everyone else, better luck next decade.
